question_text: Select the option that is related to the third word in the same way as the second word is related to the first word. Bee : Hive :: Bird : ?
explanation: A bee lives in a hive; the dwelling place of a bird is a nest. Forest, tree and sky are habitats/locations but not the specific built dwelling, so 'Nest' is the precise parallel.

question_text: Select the number that will replace the question mark (?) in the series: 5, 11, 23, 47, 95, ?
In a certain code language, 'LAMP' is written as 'NCOR'. How will 'DESK' be written in that code?
explanation: Each letter is shifted +2: L->N, A->C, M->O, P->R. Applying +2 to DESK: D->F, E->G, S->U, K->M, giving FGUM.

question_text: Pointing to a woman, Rohit said, 'She is the daughter of the only son of my grandmother.' How is the woman related to Rohit?
explanation: The only son of Rohit's grandmother is Rohit's father (assuming the line through which Rohit descends). The daughter of Rohit's father is Rohit's sister.

question_text: A man starts from his house and walks 8 m towards East, then turns left and walks 6 m, then turns left again and walks 8 m. How far and in which direction is he from his house now?
explanation: East 8 m, then North 6 m (left turn), then West 8 m (left turn) cancels the East leg. Net displacement is 6 m due North of the house.

question_text: Three of the following four numbers are alike in a certain way and one is different. Select the odd one out: 17, 23, 33, 41
explanation: 17, 23 and 41 are prime numbers. 33 = 3 x 11 is composite, so it is the odd one out.

question_text: Statements: All roses are flowers. Some flowers are red. Conclusions: I. Some roses are red. II. Some flowers are roses. Which conclusion(s) logically follow(s)?
explanation: All roses are flowers guarantees Some flowers are roses (conversion), so II follows. The 'red' flowers need not include any rose, so I is not certain.

question_text: Select the term that will replace the question mark (?) in the series: DF, GI, JL, MO, ?
explanation: Within each pair the gap is one letter (D,_,F). Each pair starts 3 letters after the previous start: D, G, J, M, P. So next pair is P and P+2 = R, i.e. PR.

question_text: In the 3x3 matrix the third column is obtained from the first two columns by the same rule in each row. Row1: 7, 2, 18. Row2: 5, 4, 18. Row3: 8, 6, ?. Find the missing number.
explanation: Rule: third = (first + second) x 2. Row1: (7+2)x2 = 18; Row2: (5+4)x2 = 18; Row3: (8+6)x2 = 28.

question_text: In a row of children facing North, Karan is 7th from the left end and 18th from the right end. How many children are there in the row?
explanation: Total = (position from left) + (position from right) - 1 = 7 + 18 - 1 = 24.

question_text: Select the option that is related to the third number in the same way as the second is related to the first. 6 : 42 :: 9 : ?
explanation: The rule is n x (n+1): 6 x 7 = 42, so 9 x 10 = 90.

question_text: Select the number that will replace the question mark (?) in the series: 4, 9, 19, 39, 79, ?
In a certain code each word is coded as the sum of the alphabet positions of its letters (A=1, B=2, ..., Z=26). In this code 'CAB' = 6 and 'FED' = 15. How is 'WING' coded?
explanation: Sum of positions: CAB = 3+1+2 = 6; FED = 6+5+4 = 15, confirming the rule. WING = W(23)+I(9)+N(14)+G(7) = 53.

question_text: If 'A + B' means 'A is the mother of B', 'A - B' means 'A is the brother of B', and 'A x B' means 'A is the father of B', then which expression shows that P is the maternal uncle of Q?
explanation: P - R means P is brother of R; R + Q means R is the mother of Q. So P is the brother of Q's mother, i.e. P is the maternal uncle of Q.

question_text: Sunil walks 9 m towards South, then turns left and walks 12 m. How far is he now from his starting point?
explanation: South 9 m and then East 12 m form the legs of a right triangle. Straight-line distance = sqrt(9^2 + 12^2) = sqrt(81+144) = sqrt(225) = 15 m.

question_text: Which one of the following words CANNOT be formed using the letters of the word 'TRANSPORTATION'?
explanation: TRANSPORTATION contains no letter 'E', so 'PRINTER' (which needs an E) cannot be formed. STATION, PORTION and TRANSPORT use only available letters.

question_text: What is the angle between the hour hand and the minute hand of a clock at 4:20?
explanation: Hour hand at 4:20 = 4x30 + 20x0.5 = 130 degrees. Minute hand at 20 = 20x6 = 120 degrees. Difference = 130 - 120 = 10 degrees.

question_text: Select the word that does not belong to the group: Copper, Iron, Bronze, Aluminium
explanation: Copper, Iron and Aluminium are pure metals (elements). Bronze is an alloy of copper and tin, so it is the odd one out.

question_text: A square sheet of paper is folded exactly in half once (one straight fold), and then two holes are punched through the folded paper. When the paper is unfolded, how many holes will be seen?
explanation: One fold creates two layers. A punch goes through both layers, producing 2 holes on unfolding. Two punches therefore produce 2 x 2 = 4 holes.

question_text: Statement: 'The library will remain closed on all national holidays.' Conclusions: I. The library is open on at least some non-holiday days. II. There is at least one national holiday in the year. Which conclusion(s) follow(s)?
explanation: A library has working days, so it must be open on some non-holiday days; I follows. The statement does not assert that any national holiday actually exists, so II is not guaranteed.

question_text: Select the option related to the third word as the second is to the first. Author : Book :: Composer : ?
explanation: An author creates a book; a composer creates a symphony (a musical composition). An orchestra, instrument or concert are involved in performance, not the work created.

question_text: Select the number that will replace the question mark (?) in the series: 0, 3, 8, 15, 24, ?
explanation: The terms are n^2 - 1 for n = 1,2,3,...: 0, 3, 8, 15, 24, and the next is 6^2 - 1 = 35.

question_text: In a code language, 'TABLE' is written as 'GZYOV'. How is 'CHAIR' written in the same code?
explanation: Each letter is replaced by its opposite in the alphabet (A<->Z, B<->Y...): T->G, A->Z, B->Y, L->O, E->V. Applying to CHAIR: C->X, H->S, A->Z, I->R, R->I, giving XSZRI.

question_text: Introducing a boy, Meena said, 'He is the son of my husband's only sister.' How is the boy related to Meena?
explanation: Meena's husband's only sister is Meena's sister-in-law. Her son is Meena's nephew.

question_text: Six friends P, Q, R, S, T and U sit in a row facing North. Q is immediately to the right of P. R is at one of the extreme ends. S sits exactly between P and T. U is immediately to the left of R, who is at the right end. Reading from left, what is the correct order?

options.A: T, S, P, Q, U, R
options.B: S, T, P, Q, U, R
options.C: T, S, Q, P, U, R
options.D: P, Q, S, T, U, R

explanation: R is at the right end (position 6) and U is immediately left of R (position 5). S is exactly between P and T, and Q is immediately right of P. The arrangement T, S, P, Q, U, R satisfies all: S sits between T and P, Q is right of P, U left of R, R at right end.

question_text: Which diagram best represents the relationship among: Doctors, Surgeons, Women?

options.B: Surgeons inside Doctors, with Women as a circle overlapping both

explanation: All surgeons are doctors, so the Surgeons circle lies inside the Doctors circle. Women can be doctors, surgeons or neither, so the Women circle overlaps both. Option B captures this exactly.

question_text: If 1 January 2023 was a Sunday, what was the day of the week on 15 August 2023? (2023 is not a leap year.)
explanation: Days from 1 Jan to 15 Aug: Jan 31+Feb 28+Mar 31+Apr 30+May 31+Jun 30+Jul 31 = 212, plus 15 = 227 days, i.e. 226 days after 1 Jan. 226 mod 7 = 2. Sunday + 2 = Tuesday.

question_text: Select the term that will replace the question mark (?) in the series: C3, F6, I9, L12, ?
explanation: Letters increase by 3: C, F, I, L, O. Numbers increase by 3: 3, 6, 9, 12, 15. So the next term is O15.

question_text: Two positions of a single dice are shown. Position 1 shows faces 1 (top), 2 (front), 3 (right). Position 2 shows faces 4 (top), 2 (front), 1 (right). Which number is opposite to 2?
explanation: In both positions face 2 is on the front, so 2 stays fixed while the dice rotates about the front-back axis. The four faces 1, 3, 4 and 6 form the ring around 2, so 2 is not opposite any of them. The only number not adjacent to 2 is 5; hence 5 is opposite 2.

question_text: Statements: All keys are locks. No lock is a door. Some doors are gates. Conclusions: I. No key is a door. II. Some gates are not locks. Which conclusion(s) follow(s)?
explanation: All keys are locks and no lock is a door, so no key can be a door; I follows. 'Some doors are gates' and 'no lock is a door' tells us those gate-doors are not locks, but other gates may be locks, so II is not certain.

question_text: Select the option related to the third number in the same way as the second is to the first. 12 : 144 :: 15 : ?
explanation: The rule is squaring: 12^2 = 144, so 15^2 = 225.

question_text: Select the number that will replace the question mark (?) in the series: 6, 13, 27, 55, 111, ?
If '+' means division, '-' means multiplication, 'x' means addition and '/' means subtraction, then the value of 18 + 6 x 4 - 3 / 5 is:
P is the son of Q. R is the daughter of Q. S is the husband of R. T is the son of S. How is P related to T?
explanation: P and R are siblings (both children of Q). R is the mother of T (R's husband S is T's father). P is the brother of T's mother, i.e. T's maternal uncle.

question_text: A boy facing West turns 135 degrees clockwise and then 90 degrees anticlockwise. Which direction is he facing now?
explanation: Facing West, 135 degrees clockwise: West -> North-West (45) -> North (90) -> North-East (135). Then 90 degrees anticlockwise from North-East goes back to North-West. So he faces North-West.

question_text: Three of the following four letter-clusters are alike in a certain way and one is different. Select the odd one out: PRT, CEG, KMO, FHK
explanation: In PRT, CEG and KMO the gaps between consecutive letters are +2, +2. In FHK the gaps are +2 (F to H) and +3 (H to K), so FHK is the odd one out.

question_text: What is the angle between the hour hand and the minute hand of a clock at 9:15?
explanation: Hour hand at 9:15 = 9x30 + 15x0.5 = 277.5 degrees. Minute hand at 15 = 90 degrees. Difference = 187.5 degrees; the smaller angle between the hands is 360 - 187.5 = 172.5 degrees.

question_text: The number 60932 is held in front of a vertical plane mirror (mirror placed to the right, so the image is laterally inverted left-to-right). Which option correctly shows its mirror image?
explanation: A vertical (left-right) mirror reverses the order of the characters left to right. Reversing 60932 gives 23906.

question_text: In each row the third number is derived from the first two by the same rule. Row1: 3, 5, 16. Row2: 4, 6, 20. Row3: 7, 2, ?. Find the missing number.
explanation: Rule: third = (first + second) x 2: Row1 (3+5)x2 = 16; Row2 (4+6)x2 = 20; Row3 (7+2)x2 = 18.

question_text: Among five friends, A is taller than B but shorter than C. D is taller than C. E is shorter than B. Who is the tallest?
explanation: Order from facts: D > C > A > B > E. Therefore D is the tallest.

question_text: Select the option related to the third word as the second is to the first. Hunger : Food :: Thirst : ?
explanation: Hunger is relieved by food; thirst is relieved by water. Water is the precise satisfier, parallel to food.

question_text: Select the number that will replace the question mark (?) in the series: 2, 3, 5, 9, 17, 33, ?
In a certain code, 'WATER' is written as 'XCWIW'. How will 'EARTH' be written in the same code?
explanation: Shifts increase by position: +1,+2,+3,+4,+5. W+1=X, A+2=C, T+3=W, E+4=I, R+5=W -> XCWIW. Apply to EARTH: E+1=F, A+2=C, R+3=U, T+4=X, H+5=M -> FCUXM.

question_text: Pointing to a lady in a photograph, Suresh said, 'She is the mother of my son's sister.' How is the lady related to Suresh?
explanation: Suresh's son's sister is Suresh's own daughter. The mother of Suresh's daughter is Suresh's wife.

question_text: If 1 January 2024 was a Monday, what was the day of the week on 2 October 2024? (2024 is a leap year.)
explanation: Days from 1 Jan to 2 Oct in leap year: 31+29+31+30+31+30+31+31+30 = 274, plus 2 = 276, i.e. 275 days after 1 Jan. 275 mod 7 = 2. Monday + 2 = Wednesday.

question_text: Three of the following four number-pairs are alike in a certain way. Select the odd one out: (3, 27), (4, 64), (5, 125), (6, 180)
explanation: In three pairs the second number is the cube of the first: 3^3=27, 4^3=64, 5^3=125. But 6^3 = 216, not 180, so (6, 180) is the odd one out.

question_text: Five people A, B, C, D and E sit around a circular table facing the centre. B is to the immediate right of A. C is second to the right of A. D is to the immediate left of A. Where does E sit?
explanation: Going clockwise (to the right) from A: A, B, C. D is immediate left of A. The five seats clockwise are A, B, C, E, D. The remaining seat for E is between C and D.

question_text: Statements: Some teachers are writers. All writers are readers. Conclusions: I. Some teachers are readers. II. All readers are teachers. Which conclusion(s) follow(s)?
explanation: Some teachers are writers and all writers are readers, so those teacher-writers are readers; I follows. There is no basis to claim all readers are teachers, so II does not follow.

question_text: In a group of 120 people, 75 can speak Hindi, 55 can speak English, and 20 can speak both. How many can speak neither language?
explanation: Speak at least one = 75 + 55 - 20 = 110. Neither = 120 - 110 = 10.

question_text: A solid cube is painted on all six faces and then cut into 64 identical smaller cubes. How many of the smaller cubes have exactly one face painted?
explanation: 64 cubes mean the cube is cut 4 x 4 x 4. Cubes with exactly one painted face are the centre faces: 6 faces x (4-2)^2 = 6 x 4 = 24.
